name: Math.Vector2

values.name: add
values.comment: Vector addition: a + b
values.type: Math.Vector2.Vec number -> Math.Vector2.Vec number -> Math.Vector2.Vec number

values.name: apply
values.type: Math.Vector2.Vec (a -> b) -> Math.Vector2.Vec a -> Math.Vector2.Vec b

values.name: ceiling
values.comment: Ceiling function, rounding up.

values.name: direction
values.comment: The normalized direction from b to a: (a - b) / |a - b|
values.type: Math.Vector2.Vec Basics.Float -> Math.Vector2.Vec Basics.Float -> Math.Vector2.Vec Basics.Float

values.name: distance
values.comment: The distance between two vectors.

values.name: distanceSquared
values.comment: The square of the distance between two vectors.

values.name: divBy
values.comment:
Divide the vector by a scalar: v / s

Shorthand for `scale (1 / s)`

values.name: dot
values.comment: The dot product of a and b

values.name: floor
values.comment: Floor function, rounding down.

values.name: fromRecord
values.comment: Construct a vector from a record.
values.type: { x : a, y : a } -> Math.Vector2.Vec a

values.name: fromTuple
values.comment: Construct a vector from a tuple.

values.name: getX
values.comment: Extract the x component of a vector.

values.name: getY
values.comment: Extract the y component of a vector.

values.name: length
values.comment: The length of the given vector: |a|

values.name: lengthSquared
values.comment: The square of the length of the given vector: |a| \* |a|

values.name: lift2
values.comment:
Lift a binary function to vectors.

    lift2 (+) (Vec 1 1) (Vec 2 2)
    --> Vec 3 3 : Vec number

Keep in mind that this comes at a cost of doing a step extra and if you need performance,
you might be better off deconstructing and applying the function manually.
values.type: (a -> b -> c) -> Math.Vector2.Vec a -> Math.Vector2.Vec b -> Math.Vector2.Vec c

values.name: lift3
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d) -> Math.Vector2.Vec a -> Math.Vector2.Vec b -> Math.Vector2.Vec c -> Math.Vector2.Vec d

values.name: map
values.comment: Map a fn to the x and y value

values.name: mapX
values.comment: Map a fn to the x value

values.name: mapY
values.comment: Map a fn to the y value

values.name: mul
values.comment: Component-wise multiplication
values.type: Math.Vector2.Vec number -> Math.Vector2.Vec number -> Math.Vector2.Vec number

values.name: negate
values.comment: Vector negation: -a

values.name: normalize
values.comment: A unit vector with the same direction as the given vector: a / |a|

values.name: round
values.comment: Round the number components to the nearest integer.

values.name: scale
values.comment: Multiply the vector by a scalar: s \* v

values.name: setX
values.comment: Update the x component of a vector, returning a new vector.

values.name: setY
values.comment: Update the y component of a vector, returning a new vector.

values.name: sub
values.comment: Vector subtraction: a - b
values.type: Math.Vector2.Vec number -> Math.Vector2.Vec number -> Math.Vector2.Vec number

values.name: toFloat
values.comment: Convert an integer vector into a float vector.

values.name: toRecord
values.comment: Convert a vector to a record.
values.type: Math.Vector2.Vec a -> { x : a, y : a }

values.name: truncate
values.comment: Truncate the number components, rounding towards zero.

values.name: vec2
values.comment: Creates a new 2-element vector with the given values.

values.name: lift2
values.comment:
Lift a binary function to vectors.

Keep in mind that this comes at a cost of doing a step extra and if you need performance,
you might be better off deconstructing and applying the function manually.
